title: Rivian Cash Flow Burn Rate and Liquidity Runway 2025
text: Rivian's free cash flow burn is estimated at -$3.2 billion for 2025, though the burn is now controlled and directed specifically toward the R2 assembly lines in Normal, Illinois. More recently, UBS expects Rivian's free cash flow burn to increase to $4 billion or more as the company ramps spending on the R2 launch and Georgia factory buildout. In Q1 2026, net cash used in operating activities hit $703 million, and free cash flow was negative $1.075 billion. Rivian exited Q3 2025 with about $7.1 billion in cash and cash equivalents, and $7.7 billion in total liquidity. More recent data from Q1 2026 shows Rivian ended the quarter with $4.83 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments, bolstered by a $1 billion Volkswagen investment. Rivian maintains a healthy liquidity cushion of $6.1 billion, supplemented by the structured $5 billion investment from the Volkswagen joint venture, with $2 billion expected to flow into Rivian's coffers throughout 2026. Operating cash flow is improving dramatically, even turning slightly positive at $26.00 million for the 2025 fiscal year, though the massive investment in the future means free cash flow remains negative. The company is burning cash on CapEx to build the business, not to cover operational losses. The expected burn must be funded against remaining resources, including the $2 billion still coming from Volkswagen and the $6.6 billion Department of Energy loan earmarked for Georgia.
